paper_title: Phytoplankton Response to Nutrient Inputs Bioassay

methods.description: Procedure for setting up a three-day bioassay incubation experiment to determine the response of phytoplankton to nutrient input from groundwater and river water, including water collection, filtering, treatment administration, and incubation.

steps.description: Local seawater from Kasitsna Bay (59.48 • N, 151.56 • W) was collected in acid cleaned, seawater rinsed carboys.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect local seawater from specified location.

steps.description: Seawater was pre-filtered to remove grazers (mesh = 105 µm).
steps.searchable_summary: Pre-filter collected seawater using a 105 µm mesh.

steps.description: Pre-filtered seawater was distributed into acid cleaned 500 mL incubation bottles.
steps.searchable_summary: Distribute pre-filtered seawater into acid-cleaned incubation bottles.

steps.description: Local river water was collected from a stream that discharges into Kasitsna Bay adjacent to the laboratory. The water was collected 5 m upstream of the area of tidal influence.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect local river water from a stream near the lab, upstream of tidal influence.

steps.description: River water was pumped through a 0.2 µm cartridge filter into an acid-cleaned carboy.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter collected river water using a 0.2 µm cartridge filter.

steps.description: Local groundwater was collected from a temporary well that screened at 0.5 m depth located above the high tide line during low tide.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect local groundwater from a temporary well at specified depth and location.
inputs: Location (temporary well near Kasitsna Bay lab)

steps.description: The groundwater was pumped through a 0.2 µm cartridge filter into an acid cleaned carboy.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter collected groundwater using a 0.2 µm cartridge filter.

steps.description: Nutrients, river water, or groundwater additions were administered to the incubation bottles according to defined treatment groups.
steps.searchable_summary: Administer nutrient solutions, filtered river water, or filtered groundwater to incubation bottles based on treatment design.

steps.description: Three replicate bottles were prepared for each time point of the experiment (0 h, 24 h, 48 h, and 72 h), for a total of 12 bottles per treatment.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare three replicate bottles for each treatment and time point.

steps.description: Bottles were incubated in a flow-through incubation tank with running seawater at ambient ocean temperature for three days on the pier of the Kasitsna Bay Laboratory.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate bottles in a flow-through tank at ambient seawater temperature for three days.

steps.description: A neutral density screen was used to attenuate natural irradiance levels by 50% to simulate light attenuation within the surface mixed layer.
steps.searchable_summary: Attenuate light during incubation using a neutral density screen.

steps.description: At each time point (0 h, 24 h, 48 h, and 72 h), three bottles were sacrificed for each treatment, and samples were collected for nutrient concentrations, chlorophyll a, and phytoplankton community composition.
steps.searchable_summary: At specified time points, collect samples for nutrient analysis, chlorophyll a, and phytoplankton community composition from replicate bottles.

steps.description: Samples were also collected for analysis of nutrient concentrations in the groundwater, river water, and seawater used in the experiment.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect samples for nutrient analysis from the source groundwater, river water, and seawater.

methods.description: Procedure for measuring salinity of source waters and calculating salinity for experimental treatment groups.

steps.description: Salinity of the groundwater, river water, and seawater was measured with a YSI 85 multiprobe.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure salinity of source water types using a multiprobe.

steps.description: The salinity of the treatment groups was calculated based on the salinity of the three water types and the proportions of those water types in each treatment group.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate salinity for each treatment group based on source water salinities and mixing proportions.

methods.description: Procedure for preparing and analyzing samples to determine nutrient concentrations.

steps.description: Nutrient samples were syringe filtered (0.2 µm) into acid cleaned, sample rinsed plastic tubes (50 mL).
steps.searchable_summary: Syringe filter nutrient samples using a 0.2 µm filter into prepared tubes.

steps.description: Filtered nutrient samples were stored frozen until analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Store filtered nutrient samples frozen.

steps.description: Samples were analyzed via colorimetric methods [24] on a flow injection autoanalyzer (FIA, Lachat Instruments Model QuickChem 8000).
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze nutrient samples using colorimetric methods on a flow injection autoanalyzer.
outputs: Nutrient concentration data (nitrate, phosphate, silicate, nitrite, ammonium)

methods.description: Procedure for collecting, extracting, and measuring chlorophyll a concentration from water samples.

steps.description: Chlorophyll a samples were collected by filtering 200 mL of sample water onto GF/F filters (Whatman) under gentle vacuum.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter water samples onto GF/F filters under vacuum to collect chlorophyll a.
outputs: GF/F filters with retained particles (including chlorophyll a)

steps.description: The filters were stored frozen at −80 • C until analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Store GF/F filters frozen at -80 °C.

steps.description: Chlorophyll a was extracted from the filters for 24 h in 90% acetone at 4 • C.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract chlorophyll a from filters using 90% acetone at 4 °C for 24 hours.

steps.description: The fluorescence of the extract was measured on a Turner Fluorometer (Turner Designs TF700).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure the fluorescence of the chlorophyll a extract using a fluorometer.

methods.description: Procedure for determining the absolute and relative abundance of phytoplankton genera by microscopy and cell counting.

steps.description: Samples were preserved with 4% formalin.
steps.searchable_summary: Preserve phytoplankton samples using 4% formalin.

steps.description: Preserved samples were stored in the dark at 4 • C until analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Store preserved phytoplankton samples in the dark at 4 °C.

steps.description: Cells were settled from 50 mL aliquots in Utermöhl settling chambers.
steps.searchable_summary: Settle cells from sample aliquots in Utermöhl settling chambers.

steps.description: Phytoplankton genera were enumerated using an Olympus XI 70 inverted microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: Enumerate phytoplankton genera using an inverted microscope.

steps.description: A minimum of 500 cells per sample were counted at 200× magnification.
steps.searchable_summary: Count a minimum number of cells per sample at a specified magnification.

steps.description: Uncertainty was estimated in two ways: comparing counts by two investigators and comparing counts from different fields within the same settled sample.
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate counting uncertainty using inter-observer comparison or within-sample field comparison.
